question_text: A box of mass 20 kg is being dragged across a rough wooden floor. The person pulling the rope must apply a force of 80 N to keep the box moving at constant speed. Which statement best explains why the pulling force must be at least 80 N?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravity pulls the box downward with a force greater than 80 N

options.label: B
options.text: Friction acts as a contact force opposing the motion, and the pulling force must overcome it to maintain constant speed

options.label: C
options.text: The weight of the box creates tension in the rope that opposes the pull

options.label: D
options.text: Magnetic forces between the box and floor resist the motion

explanation: Friction is a contact force between the box and floor that opposes motion. Since the box moves at constant speed, the forces are balanced—the 80 N pull equals the 80 N friction force. Option A confuses gravity (which acts downward); option C incorrectly links weight to rope tension; option D is wrong because magnetic forces don't significantly affect everyday objects on floors.
